channel.title: Posts on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Interpreter vs Visitor Pattern in C#: Key Differences Explained
item.description: Compare the interpreter vs visitor pattern in C# with code examples, use cases, and guidance on choosing the right pattern for tree processing. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/26/interpreter-vs-visitor-pattern-in-c-key-differences-explained

item.title: Open-Sourcing the LSP Client API in IntelliJ IDEA 2026.2
item.description: Language Server Protocol (LSP) solves a real problem: before, each IDE or editor had to implement support for a specific language separately. A language server puts that work in one place and exposes it through a common protocol, providing completion, formatting, documentation, and related features. That common protocol is only a part of the IDE [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2026/06/open-sourcing-the-lsp-client-api-in-intellij-idea-2026-2/

item.title: Passing Context Between Agents in Multi-Agent A2A Systems
item.description: Introduction Multi-agent systems are becoming the go-to architecture for complex AI workloads. Instead of one monolithic agent doing everything, teams decompose capabilities into specialized agents вҖ” each with its own domain expertise, deployment pipeline, and release cadence. This approach mirrors the microservice patterns that ha Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/a2a-context-passing-multi-agent-systems

item.title: Your agent already has a plan
item.description: If an agent isn't doing the right thing, the obvious move is to make the docs clearer. Add a tip, spell out the correct command, describe the right approach more prominently. You do all of that, and the agent still ignores it. It does what it had already decided to do. The tip wasn't ignored because it was unclear, it was ignored beca Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/your-agent-already-has-a-plan

item.title: Save the Date: Pure Virtual C++ 2026
item.description: Pure Virtual C++ is back! For the 7th year running, we're hosting a free, one-day virtual conference for the whole C++ community. Whether you joined us back in 2020 when we first launched or you're discovering the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/save-the-date-pure-virtual-cpp-2026

item.title: EF Core vs Dapper in .NET: When to Use Each
item.description: EF Core vs Dapper in .NET 10 -- compare performance, query control, migrations, learning curve, and a decision matrix to pick the right ORM for your project. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/25/ef-core-vs-dapper-in-net-when-to-use-each

item.title: How to Generate an SBOM for Container Workflows
item.description: Learn when, where, and how to generate SBOMs for container images. Covers build-time vs. post-build approaches, quality criteria, and CI/CD integration. Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/sbom-generation-for-container-workflows/

item.title: Learn from Microsoft: Transform software development through an agentic platform
item.description: AI is already changing how teams build software, and the biggest shift goes beyond writing code faster. ItвҖҷs reshaping the full software lifecycle, from planning and development to security, operations, and modernization. Inside Microsoft, we build and run software at massive scale, across every kind of codebase, and we put these agentic too Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/learn-from-microsoft-transform-software-development-through-an-agentic-platform

item.title: Retrofit, donвҖҷt rebuild: Agentic overlays for transforming legacy enterprise services
item.description: In this technical collaboration between AWS and the authors, we present a pragmatic solution: agentic overlays. Agentic overlays are thin wrapper layers that transform traditional REST-based services into agents capable of participating in A2A interactions. They also expose REST APIs as tools compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Together, they let enterprises add A2A capabilities to existing REST services without rewriting business logic, without duplicating code, and without running parallel infrastructures.

item.title: Optimize model training on Amazon SageMaker AI with NVIDIA Blackwell
item.description: This post shows you how to configure training jobs on Amazon SageMaker AI to get the most out of BlackwellвҖҷs architecture on AWS. You learn how to select batch sizes and sequence lengths that take advantage of BlackwellвҖҷs expanded memory, choose the right precision format for your model size (1B to 64B parameters), and apply activation checkpointing strategically. By the end, you have a practical framework for tuning your training configuration and launching distributed training jobs on P6-B200 instances.

item.title: Implementing super resolution by deploying SeedVR2 on Amazon SageMaker AI
item.description: In this post, we demonstrate how to implement video upscaling using SeedVR2 on SageMaker AI. We cover the solution architecture, walk through the deployment steps, and show performance comparisons that highlight the quality improvements and processing efficiency you can achieve. By the end of this post, youвҖҷll have the practical knowledge needed to implement this super resolution solution. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/implementing-super-resolution-by-deploying-seedvr2-on-amazon-sagemaker-ai/

item.title: Build self-service AWS Health analytics to find actionable health insights with AI agents powered by Amazon Bedrock
item.description: In this post, we show you how to build Chaplin (Customer Health and Planned Lifecycle Intelligence Nexus), an open source solution that uses AI agents exposed through the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to provide self-service health event analytics. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/build-self-service-aws-health-analytics-to-find-actionable-health-insights-with-ai-agents-powered-by-amazon-bedrock/

item.title: Building agentic AI applications with a modern data mesh strategy on AWS
item.description: This post shows how to build a governed, serverless data mesh on AWS that provides the secure, scalable data foundation production agentic AI requires. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/building-agentic-ai-applications-with-a-modern-data-mesh-strategy-on-aws/

item.title: EU Cyber Resilience Act: Overview, Requirements, and Timelines
item.description: Learn what the EU Cyber Resilience Act requires, including SBOM mandates, vulnerability reporting, and compliance deadlines for container teams. Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/eu-cyber-resilience-act-overview/

item.title: The Dev Containers Story: Introducing EelApi for Plugin Authors
item.description: Modern development has shifted one old IDE paradigm significantly: Now, not only is it possible that a project is not hosted on the same physical or remote machine as your IDE instance, it could even be that both share the same host but are separated from one another inside isolated environments. If you are a [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2026/06/the-dev-containers-story-introducing-eelapi-for-plugin-authors/

item.title: The real winner of CursorвҖҷs $60B acquisition wonвҖҷt be AI coding assistants
item.description: When news broke that SpaceX would acquire CursorвҖҷs parent company, Anysphere, in a reported $60 billion all-stock deal, most of the discussion centered around AI. This was another milestone and enormous valuation, and signal that AI is still bringing enormous disruption. Those reactions arenвҖҷt wrong but they can overshadow the bigger story. The real significance [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/qodana/2026/06/cursor-s-60b-acquisition/

item.title: Introducing the Recommended Agent in AI Chat with Codex as the current choice
item.description: JetBrains AI supports multiple coding agents, including Junie, Codex, Claude Agent, and any ACP-compatible agent you bring yourself. Previously, AI users in JetBrains IDEs started in Chat mode and had to choose an agent themselves. As models became more advanced, agents became more capable and their adoption grew. We recognize that agents help users achieve [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2026/06/codex-is-now-the-recommended-agent-in-jetbrains-ai/

item.title: SSH Connections Are Moving to JetBrains Daemon in the Toolbox App 3.6 EAP
item.description: Starting with the Toolbox App 3.6 Early Access Program (EAP), SSH connections to remote environments use the JetBrains Daemon (jetbrainsd) by default. WeвҖҷre making this change available in the EAP first because jetbrainsd is becoming the shared background service for JetBrains tools. Moving SSH there helps us validate the new connection flow before using it [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/toolbox-app/2026/06/ssh-connections-are-moving-to-jetbrains-daemon-in-the-toolbox-app-3-6-eap/

item.title: Your AI Agent Keeps Missing The Real Bottleneck. JetBrains Rider Can Fix It Now.
item.description: HereвҖҷs a case worth pondering: your app freezes for ten seconds, and you ask an AI agent whatвҖҷs wrong. What does it actually do? For a long time the honest answer was: it rummages through your code and takes a wild guess. A snapshot taken by a profiler tool is runtime evidence. It knows exactly [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/dotnet/2026/06/25/performance-profiling-agent-skill-in-rider/

item.title: Raymond's hot take on Hainanese chicken
item.description: "If we give it no flavor, we can say that it's really subtle." Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260625-01/?p=112469

item.title: The case of the DLL that was not present in memory despite not being formally unloaded, part 1
item.description: The team responsible for shell32.dll received a bug saying that they were responsible for a large number of crashes in a particular third party program. Opening the crash dumps showed the clear signs of a stack overflow: # Child-SP RetAddr Call Site Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260625-00/?p=112467

item.title: Which Azure Cosmos DB Role Does My App Need?
item.description: In the previous post in the series, we covered the security decisions you make on day one. In this part, we will talk about how to give your app access to Cosmos DB data, using roles and a managed identity instead of keys Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/which-azure-cosmos-db-role-does-my-app-need

item.title: Visitor Design Pattern in C#: Complete Guide with Examples
item.description: Master the visitor design pattern in C# with code examples, double dispatch, and best practices for separating algorithms from object structures. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/25/visitor-design-pattern-in-c-complete-guide-with-examples

item.title: The Unglamorous Side of Rust Web Development
item.description: This is a guest post by Mateusz MaДҮkowski and Marek Grzelak, co-maintainers of cot.rs and speakers at Rustikon 2026. You can watch their full talk here. In the very beginning, all we wanted to do was build a JSON API. After doing that a few times in Rust, we noticed a recurring pattern. Every new [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/rust/2026/06/25/rust-web-development-2026/

item.title: Big Thinkers: Guido van Rossum вҖ“ How Python Became the Connective Tissue of Modern Infrastructure and AI
item.description: Few programming languages have crossed as many technology boundaries as Python. It powers machine learning platforms, cloud automation, observability tooling, scientific research, infrastructure orchestration, cybersecurityвҖҰ Link to article: https://build5nines.com/big-thinkers-guido-van-rossum-how-python-became-the-connective-tissue-of-modern-infrastructure-and-ai/

item.title: Our Research on Membership Inference Attacks and Preventing Privacy Leaks
item.description: Imagine thereвҖҷs a stranger out there who has nothing but API access to your chatbot. They are interested in knowing whether a specific patient, employee, or customer appears in the data you trained it on. Without breaching the database or stealing backups, this person can theoretically figure out this information with carefully crafted prompts and [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/research/2026/06/membership-inference/

item.title: Explicit Lazy Imports Are Coming to Python 3.15
item.description: A while ago at PyCon US 2026, I had the pleasure of listening to the Python Steering Council give updates about new features that are being added in Python 3.15. One that stood out was explicit lazy imports (via PEP 810), which defer module loading until first use. I am curious to see how this [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/06/explicit-lazy-imports-are-coming-to-python-3-15/

item.title: How agents are transforming work
item.description: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/how-agents-are-transforming-work

item.title: Testing with EF Core in C#: In-Memory vs SQLite for Unit Tests
item.description: Learn EF Core unit testing in .NET 10 -- UseInMemoryDatabase vs SQLite in-memory, IDbContextFactory for isolation, seeding test data, and xUnit patterns. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/24/testing-with-ef-core-in-c-inmemory-vs-sqlite-for-unit-tests

item.title: Azure Functions MCP Extension: What's New at Build 2026
item.description: The Azure Functions MCP extension provides simple abstractions to help you build and host MCP servers without having to learn the protocol details yourself. You can use the triggers and bindings model to expose any function as an MCP tool, resource, or prompt. Since its initial preview, the extension has grown from supporting a single trigger Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/functions-mcp-updates-build-2026

item.title: Huntington Bank: Redacting sensitive data from 400M+ documents with AWS
item.description: In this post, we walk through how Huntington built a scalable AWS solution to detect and redact Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Payment Card Industry (PCI) data from over 400 million documents, reducing processing time from years to just a few months while achieving 95%+ redaction accuracy. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/huntington-bank-redacting-sensitive-data-from-400m-documents-with-aws/

item.title: Build a healthcare appointment agent with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: In this post, you will learn how to build a voice agent that handles appointment reminder conversations using Amazon Nova 2 Sonic and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The agent authenticates patients by voice, manages appointments (confirm, cancel, or reschedule), collects pre-visit health information, and escalates to human staff when needed. You handle routine calls at scale, which can help reduce no-show rates. This sample focuses on the agentic side of the problem: voice conversation and tool orchestration.

item.title: AI-powered BI with Snowflake and Amazon Quick
item.description: In this post, you will learn how to build an end-to-end integration between Snowflake semantic views and Amazon Quick. The sample data is user review data for a media company. You start by loading movie review data from Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) into Snowflake, define a semantic view in SQL to add business meaning, explore it with natural-language queries through Cortex Analyst, and then generate an Amazon Quick dataset and dashboard.

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.126
item.description: Learn what is new in Visual Studio Code 1.126 Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/visual-studio-code-1.126

item.title: How Loka Built a Natural, Low-Latency Voice Agent with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: In this post, we demonstrate the architecture and approach Loka used to solve a common frustration: robotic, slow voice assistants that cause customers to hang up, damaging brand reputation and driving up support costs. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/how-loka-built-a-natural-low-latency-voice-agent-with-amazon-nova-2-sonic/

item.title: When the model has never seen your code
item.description: This is the fifth article in a series about Agent Experience (AX): the practice of making AI coding agents work correctly with your technology. The series covers what you can and can't control in the agent stack, how to measure whether your extensions are helping or hurting, and how to iterate toward better outcomes. Everyt Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/when-the-model-has-never-seen-your-code

item.title: Resilience Patterns Can Make Your System Less Resilient
item.description: You wanted your system to be resilient, so you followed the standard advice. You added retries.You added circuit breakers.You added fallbacks. But now your system is less resilient. Not because those patterns are bad. They are not. The problem is they are doing exactly what you told them to do. YouTube Check out myВ YouTube channel,В whereвҖҰВ Read More В»Resilience Patterns Can Make Your System Less Resilient The post Resilience Patterns Can Make Your System Less Resilient appeared first on CodeOpinion.

item.title: Kotlin Toolchain 0.11: The Next Step for Amper
item.description: Amper 0.11.0 is out, and you will notice a shift in the product branding immediately. If you missed the KotlinConf keynote (watch the recording), hereвҖҷs the headline: Amper has evolved into the Kotlin Toolchain and is now Alpha! This release brings that transition to life, alongside the ability to publish JVM libraries, new plugin development [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/amper/2026/06/kotlin-toolchain-0-11/

item.title: Interpreter Pattern Best Practices in C#: Code Organization and Maintainability
item.description: Interpreter pattern best practices in C# for clean grammar design, expression tree management, and avoiding common DSL pitfalls. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/24/interpreter-pattern-best-practices-in-c-code-organization-and-maintainability

item.title: Azure Bicep Has a Plan Mode: Use It On Your Next Production Deployment
item.description: Infrastructure as code makes cloud environments repeatable, but repeatability does not automatically make deployments safe. A small Bicep change can have consequences far beyond theвҖҰ Link to article: https://build5nines.com/azure-bicep-has-a-plan-mode-use-it-on-your-next-production-deployment/

item.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
item.description: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce JalapeГұo, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip

item.title: YouTrack Helpdesk Now Includes Customer Groups
item.description: YouTrack 2026.2 introduces customer groups in YouTrack Helpdesk, helping B2B support teams tailor the support experience to different customers. Teams can manage tickets by company, team, or organization, maintain different SLAs, assign support agents to specific groups, and apply customer-specific rules. Multiple reporters from the same group can share tickets, follow updates, comment, and stay [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/youtrack/2026/06/youtrack-helpdesk-now-includes-customer-groups/

item.title: Streamline C++ Code Intelligence Setup in Copilot CLI
item.description: C++ developers can now bring IDE-style semantic intelligence into Copilot CLI with less setup. The C++ language server plugin supports richer code navigation, diagnostics, symbol understanding, and code changes by using the same build context your compiler does. That build cont Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cppblog/streamline-c-code-intelligence-setup-in-copilot-cli

item.title: EF Core Performance Best Practices in .NET 10
item.description: Optimize EF Core performance in .NET 10 -- AsNoTracking, compiled queries, split queries, bulk operations, N+1 fixes, and logging slow queries with Serilog. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/23/ef-core-performance-best-practices-in-net-10

item.title: How GPT-5 helped immunologist Derya Unutmaz solve a 3-year-old mystery
item.description: GPT-5 Pro helped solve a 3-year-old immunology mystery, offering insights into T cell behavior. The breakthrough could support cancer and autoimmune research. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-immunology-mystery

item.title: What is an SBOM (and Why CanвҖҷt You Ship Without One)?
item.description: In Omdia's 2026 software supply chain security report, 73% of organizations that generate SBOMs say they enable more efficient vulnerability mitigation, yet 86% still find the generation process challenging. That gap between recognized value and operational difficulty is where most teams are stuck. For teams building and securing containerized applications, understanding what an SBOM is,... Link to article: https://www.docker.com/blog/what-is-an-sbom/

item.title: Build a protein research copilot with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: This post shows you how to build a conversational protein research assistant that combines three capabilities: Natural language query parsing to extract structured search parameters, vector similarity search over protein embeddings using a specialized language model and ai-generated scientific summaries of search results. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/build-a-protein-research-copilot-with-amazon-bedrock-agentcore/

item.title: Add vs Stage and Register
item.description: As previously mentioned to 'install' a package, as people think of the term, Windows actually performs two separate operations: staging and registration. To oversimplify: Staging puts t Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/insidemsix/add-vs-stage-and-register

item.title: Shared infrastructure, isolated tenants: Pool model multi-tenancy with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: In this post, you will learn patterns for implementing production-ready multi-tenant systems using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. You will see these patterns demonstrated through healthcare AI agents that serve multiple clinics and hospitals. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/shared-infrastructure-isolated-tenants-pool-model-multi-tenancy-with-amazon-bedrock-agentcore/

item.title: Helping build shared standards for advanced AI
item.description: OpenAI helps build shared standards for advanced AI, supporting evaluation frameworks, safety practices, and global cooperation through the Appia Foundation. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/helping-build-shared-standards-for-advanced-ai

item.title: Memento Pattern Real-World Example in C#: Complete Implementation
item.description: Memento pattern real-world example in C# -- build a document editor with undo/redo, state history, and bounded memory management. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/23/memento-pattern-realworld-example-in-c-complete-implementation

item.title: Going beyond text in Microsoft 365 Copilot - Introducing SharePoint Copilot Apps
item.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot is brilliant at working with language. But work isn't only words, sometimes you don't want to describe what you need in a back-and-forth chat - you want to see it, touch it, and act on it right w Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/microsoft365dev/going-beyond-text-in-microsoft-365-copilot-introducing-sharepoint-copilot-apps

item.title: Conditional & Iterative Deployments with Azure Bicep
item.description: Every team eventually reaches the point where their Infrastructure as Code stops being вҖңa few clean resource declarationsвҖқ and starts becoming a living model ofвҖҰ Link to article: https://build5nines.com/conditional-iterative-deployments-with-azure-bicep/

item.title: Avoiding ToString() allocations with StringBuilder.MoveChunks: Exploring the .NET 11 preview - Part 3
item.description: In this post I take a short look at the new MoveChunks API, explore how it works, what it's for, and how the new API provides opportunities for public APIs Link to article: https://andrewlock.net/exploring-the-dotnet-11-preview-3-avoiding-tostring-allocations-with-stringbuilder-movechunks/

item.title: How Omio is building the future of conversational travel
item.description: Discover how Omio uses OpenAI to power conversational travel experiences, accelerate product development, and transform into an AI-native company. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/omio

item.title: SQL Server as a Document Database вҖ” and why you want that!
item.description: While Polecat is pretty new, itвҖҷs based on over a decade of experience and usage patterns established by the Marten library (and also shares a ton of common infrastructure code as well). Polecat is also backed up by JasperFx Software and weвҖҷre available for either consulting or support agreements for Polecat usage. If youвҖҷre a вҖҰ Continue reading SQL Server as a Document Database вҖ” and why you wantВ that! вҶ’

item.title: EF Core Relationships in C#: One-to-Many, Many-to-Many, and One-to-One
item.description: Configure EF Core relationships in .NET 10 -- one-to-many, one-to-one, many-to-many with join entity, cascade delete, shadow properties, and fluent API. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/22/ef-core-relationships-in-c-onetomany-manytomany-and-onetoone

item.title: Building pay-per-intelligence for AI agents: How Ampersend uses Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Payments
item.description: In this post, you will learn how Ampersend built a pay-per-intelligence routing layer on top of Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Payments. AI agents autonomously route tasks to the most effective model, pay per request, and operate within spending budgets. You will also see how the two-hop payment pattern works end-to-end and how to get started with your own implementation. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/building-pay-per-intelligence-for-ai-agents-how-ampersend-uses-amazon-bedrock-agentcore-payments/

item.title: Meet your agent harness and claw
item.description: Part 1 of Build your own claw and agent harness with Microsoft Agent Framework. In the Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/meet-your-agent-harness-and-claw

item.title: Build your own claw and agent harness with Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: What does it take to build your own "claw" - a capable, CLI-style agent that can plan, use tools, remember things, and safely act on your behalf? Coding agents and assistants like these can feel like magic, but underneath they are an agent harness: a loop around a language model wired up with tools, planning, memory, approvals, and ob Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/agent-framework/build-your-own-claw-and-agent-harness-with-microsoft-agent-framework

item.title: Embed the world: Multimodal AI for searchable aerial imagery at scale
item.description: In this post, we walk through the problem space, our architecture on Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch Serverless, the evaluation methodology we built on OpenStreetMap ground truth, four experiments that compared embedding models, fusion strategies, captioning, and search methods, and the practical guidance you can apply when building a similar system. YouвҖҷll learn which design choices move the needle for geospatial semantic search, including why Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings delivered the highest F1 scores across both benchmark queries in our evaluation.

item.title: Running ComfyUI workflows on Amazon SageMaker AI processing jobs
item.description: In this post, we walk you through how to deploy ComfyUI workflows on Amazon SageMaker AI processing jobs to generate hundreds of high-quality images in a single batch. You learn how to set up the infrastructure using AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK), configure GPU-accelerated processing, and automate image generation at scale. You can then adapt this solution to your ComfyUI workflows specific to your needs. We will guide you through a practical, step-by-step process to automate ComfyUI workflows to generate hundreds of high-quality images in a single batch empowering you to scale your creative pipeline.

item.title: How to Win a Hackathon: Notes From the Judging Table
item.description: At the JetBrains x Codex Hackathon, I spent two days watching teams build and then pitch their projects. The thing that decided most of the winners wasnвҖҷt just the previous twenty-four hours of work. It was the few minutes they spent presenting it. A strong project with a confusing demo loses to a simpler project [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/ai/2026/06/how-to-win-a-hackathon-notes-from-the-judging-table/

item.title: Models don't have preferences, they have context
item.description: You open a fresh chat, type "What framework should I use for a web app?", and the model says "React." You screenshot it, share it, and write "Claude prefers React." It gets engagement. People nod along. A few reply with their own results. And now we have a consensus: Claude prefers React. Except it doesn't. The model doesn't prefer anything Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/blog/models-dont-have-preferences-they-have-context

item.title: In memory of the man who put red and green squiggles under words
item.description: I recently learned of the passing of someone whose work nearly everybody knows, but nobody knows his name. Tony Krueger is remembered in Wikipedia as the person who ported the game Chip's Challenge to Windows for the Windows Entertainment Pack.ГӮЕЎ But that's probably not t Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20260622-00/?p=112451

item.title: How to Use Deep Agents with Azure Cosmos DB вҖ“ Plan, act, and verify against operational data
item.description: Deep Agents is an agent harness built on LangGraph, for agents that need to work through a task over many steps instead of a single LLM call. The agent runs tools, looks at the results, and uses that to Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/deep-agents-to-plan-act-verify-against-operational-data

item.title: How We Measure the ROI of JetBrains IDEs
item.description: Organizations already spend hundreds of thousands of dollars on software, so itвҖҷs only natural that when they evaluate new paid tools, one question is top of mind: вҖңWill this actually pay off?вҖқ Our ROI (return on investment) calculator was designed to help estimate the potential gains of JetBrains IDEs and AI Ultimate subscriptions. The ROI [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/research/2026/06/how-we-measure-the-roi-of-jetbrains-ides/

item.title: When to Use Interpreter Pattern in C#: Decision Guide with Examples
item.description: Learn when to use the interpreter pattern in C# with decision criteria, DSL scenarios, and code examples for grammar-based processing. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/22/when-to-use-interpreter-pattern-in-c-decision-guide-with-examples

item.title: AWS Image Builder Plugin for TeamCity
item.description: Cloud build agents are one of those CI/CD features that feel almost magical when everything works well. Your TeamCity server can scale build capacity up when the queue gets busy, then wind it back down when the rush is over. You get extra power exactly when you need it, without keeping machines idle the rest [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/teamcity/2026/06/teamcity-aws-ami-builder/

item.title: Generate a Kiota client at build time from an ASP.NET Core OpenAPI file
item.description: In a previous post, I explained how to generate the OpenAPI document during the ASP.NET Core build and commit it to the repository. That gives you a versioned API contract that is easy to review in pull requests. In this post, let's go one step further: use that generated OpenAPI file as the input for Kiota, so your typed .NET client is also generated during build. The goal is simple: The server producesвҖҰ

item.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
item.description: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/daybreak-securing-the-world

item.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
item.description: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/patch-the-planet

item.title: Retirement of Azure DevOps issuer in Workload identity federation service connections
item.description: We are announcing the deprecation of the Azure DevOps issuer in workload identity federation (WIF) service connections, with planned retirement on July 1, 2027. The Azure DevOps issuer uses the https://vstoken.dev.azure.com prefix in federated credentials. This change is part of Microsoft's bro Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/devops/retirement-of-azure-devops-issuer-in-workload-identity-federation-service-connections

item.title: PHP Version Migration That DoesnвҖҷt Break Everything
item.description: PHP Version Migration Demo PHP powers 71.8% of all websites with a known server-side language. It is a living language that underpins a significant portion of the modern web and one that keeps getting better. For example, EKSNEKS notes that migrating from PHP 7 to PHP 8 alone can deliver 18вҖ“42% performance gains depending on [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/qodana/2026/06/php-version-migration/

item.title: Copilot ДәЕ“ВЁ JetBrains IDEДҸЕәЕЎДәЕ ЕёДҚЖ’ЛқДҮвҖәВҙДҮвҖ“В°ГӨВёЕҪ Claude ГӨЛқЕ“ГӨВёЕҹ Agent ДҮГӨЕҫвҖәДҮвҖ“ЕЎГ©ЛҳвҖһДҚВ§ЛҶ
item.description: ДҮЕ“Е№ДҮЕ№Д„ДҮвҖәВҙДҮвҖ“В°ГӨВёЕҹ GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs ДәВёЕҡДҮДҪГӨЕҹвҖ ДәЕ»ЕЎДҮДҪДҚвҖЎЕһ GitHub Г§ЕЎвҖһГ§ЕҘвҖһГ§ЕҘвҖЎДәвҖҷЕ’ГӨЕәГӨВёЕЎГ§ЕҹВ§ agent Г§ЕЎвҖһДҮвҖқЕ»ДҮЕ’ДҸЕәЕ’ДҚЕҪЕ ГӨЛқВ ДәЕ»ГӨЕҘДҪДәЕ“ВЁ Copilot CLI ГӨЕәЕЎДҚЕ»ГӨВёВӯДҮЕҪвҖҷГ©ЛңЕёДәвҖҷЕ’ДәЕәвҖўДәЕ»ЕәДҮЕӣЛҶДҮЕ»ДҸЕәЕ’ДәЕәвҖўДәвҖҰДҪГӨЕҹвҖ ДәвҖҰВЁДҮвҖ“В°Г§ЕЎвҖһ agent ДҚВ°Ж’ДҚЕ»вҖўДҮвҖ”ДҪДәЕјвҖ”ДҮвҖҳЛңДҚЕҡДҚВ§вҖ ДәвҖәЕҫДҸЕәЕ’ДәЕЎЕӣДәВ°вҖ Claude ГӨЛқЕ“ГӨВёЕҹ agent ДҮГӨЕҫвҖәДҮвҖ“ЕЎДәВёЕҡДәвҖҰДҪДәвҖҰЕ№ДәЕәвӮ¬Г©ЛҳвҖһДҚВ§ЛҶДғвӮ¬вҖҡ ДҮВӯВӨДәВӨвҖ“ДҸЕәЕ’ДҮЕ“Е№ДҮЕ№Д„ДҮвҖәВҙДҮвҖ“В°ДҚЕјЛңДәЕ’вҖҰДәЕӨДҮВЁД„ДәЕҫвҖ№Г©вӮ¬вҖ°ДҮвҖ№Е Дәв„ўВЁДә Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/java-ch/new-features-claude-as-agent-provider-preview

item.title: Codex-maxxing for long-running work
item.description: Learn how Jason Liu uses Codex to preserve context, manage complex projects, and help work continue beyond a single prompt. Link to article: https://openai.com/index/codex-maxxing-long-running-work

item.title: Database Performance: Making Entity Framework Queries Faster
item.description: The article discusses the advantages of using compiled queries in Entity Framework, especially for frequently executed and complex queries. Link to article: https://dotnettips.com/2026/06/21/database-performance-making-entity-framework-queries-faster/

item.title: Weekly Recap: Entity Framework Core, the Memento Pattern, and C# Design Patterns [Jun 2026]
item.description: This week digs into Entity Framework Core from setup through CRUD operations and schema migrations, plus a deep run on the Memento pattern and other C# design patterns like Interpreter and Mediator. New videos cover tackling tech debt, build vs buy decisions, and growing into senior and leadership roles. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/21/weekly-recap-entity-framework-core-the-memento-pattern-and-c-design-patterns-jun-2026

item.title: EF Core Migrations in .NET: Managing Your Database Schema
item.description: Complete guide to EF Core migrations in .NET 10 -- add, apply, squash, seed data with UseSeeding, revert, and run migrations safely in CI/CD pipelines. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/20/ef-core-migrations-in-net-managing-your-database-schema

item.title: How to Implement Interpreter Pattern in C#: Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: How to implement the interpreter pattern in C# with step-by-step code examples for expression parsing, AST construction, and evaluation. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/20/how-to-implement-interpreter-pattern-in-c-stepbystep-guide

item.title: The Modular Monolith Boundary I Couldn't Take Back
item.description: We built the system exactly the way a modular monolith is supposed to be built, and that's how I ended up with two modules I couldn't pull apart a year later. Here's how a reasonable boundary decision turns into one you can't undo, and the early signals I walked right past. Link to article: https://www.milanjovanovic.tech/blog/the-modular-monolith-boundary-i-couldnt-take-back

item.title: EF Core CRUD Operations in C#: Create, Read, Update, Delete
item.description: Master EF Core CRUD in C# with .NET 10 -- learn AddAsync, SaveChangesAsync, FindAsync, Where queries, tracked updates, and bulk ExecuteUpdate/ExecuteDelete. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/19/ef-core-crud-operations-in-c-create-read-update-delete

item.title: Kafka Support Improvements in Wolverine 6.13
item.description: Wolverine 6.13 dropped yesterday with quite a few refinements to our existing integration with Kafka in Wolverine. We had, of course, probably fallen into the trap of just trying to make Kafka behave like a Rabbit MQ analogue. It was already on my radar to get enable more idiomatic Kafka capabilities, and a community request вҖҰ Continue reading Kafka Support Improvements in WolverineВ 6.13 вҶ’ Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.com/2026/06/19/kafka-support-improvements-in-wolverine-6-13/

item.title: Introducing Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is now generally available. In this post, we walk through what makes Web Search on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore different, why it matters, and how to wire it in with a few lines of code. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/introducing-web-search-on-amazon-bedrock-agentcore/

item.title: Accelerate campaign workflow with insights from Adobe Marketing Agent for Amazon Quick
item.description: This post shows how to enable Adobe Marketing Agent for Amazon Quick using a Model Context Protocol (MCP). We walk you through how to configure the integration, authenticate using your Adobe credentials, and get the latest insights in Amazon Quick. The sample workflow returns audience rankings, loyalty segment summaries, journey usage, and conflict recommendations. Link to article: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/accelerate-campaign-workflow-with-insights-from-adobe-marketing-agent-for-amazon-quick/

item.title: Bamboo End of Life: How to Prepare and Choose the Right CI/CD Replacement
item.description: If your team is using Bamboo, youвҖҷve probably seen the news: Bamboo Data Center is being retired as part of AtlassianвҖҷs broader Data Center transition strategy. Support will continue for several years, but many teams have already started thinking about the next step. Whether youвҖҷre considering Bamboo Cloud or moving to an entirely new CI/CD [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/teamcity/2026/06/bamboo-end-of-life/

item.title: Memento Pattern Best Practices in C#: Code Organization and Maintainability
item.description: Memento pattern best practices in C# for clean state management, memory optimization, and avoiding common pitfalls with undo/redo systems. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/06/19/memento-pattern-best-practices-in-c-code-organization-and-maintainability

item.title: Structuring IntelliJ Plugins with Optional Content Modules
item.description: What if one part of your plugin should load only when a specific IDE functionality is available? Plugin Model v2 is now available as an experimental way to structure, package, and build plugins, and to support such scenarios in a future-proof way. Its primary use case is in Split Mode (Remote Development) plugins. In this [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/platform/2026/06/structuring-intellij-plugins-with-optional-content-modules/

item.title: YouTrack Security Update: Upgrade Required for YouTrack Server
item.description: WeвҖҷre sharing this update to inform YouTrack administrators about several security vulnerabilities that were recently identified and fixed in YouTrack. For YouTrack Cloud users, this post is purely informational вҖ“ YouTrack Cloud has already been patched and no action is required.В For YouTrack Server administrators, we recommend upgrading to one of the fixed versions listed [вҖҰ] Link to article: https://blog.jetbrains.com/youtrack/2026/06/youtrack-security-update-youtrack-server-upgrade-required/

item.title: Separating Deterministic Extraction from AI Inference in Industrial Summarization
item.description: Introduction: When Valid JSON Is Not Enough 100% of our outputs were valid JSON. 0% met the data contract. We prototyped an LLM-powered system that converts operational shift logs into structured summaries consumed by dashboards, compliance workflows, and incoming crews. The output schema required specific operational sections bec Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/ise/separating-deterministic-extraction-from-ai-inference

item.title: What 50,000 Runs of a 5-Line Eval Taught Us
item.description: How AI coding models calibrate effort, token cost, and tool use on even the simplest task, and what that means for model selection and cost. Read the full article Link to article: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/vscode-blog/what-50,000-runs-of-a-5-line-eval-taught-us
